---
title: "Lauren and Jillian - Random Forest"
output: html_document
date: "2024-11-14"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# install packages and load libraries
library(ModelMetrics)
library(parallel)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(leaps)
library(ggplot2)
library(glmnet)
library(ISLR)
library(dials)
library(tidymodels)
library(randomForest)
library(ranger)
library(vip)
library(parsnip)
library(rsample)
```

```{r}
# load data
final_train_lags <- read_csv("/data/accounts/fa24/garretthayden/DSCI445/FinalProject2/Data/final_train_lags_lasso_rf.csv")

final_test_lags <- read_csv("/data/accounts/fa24/garretthayden/DSCI445/FinalProject2/Data/final_test_lags_lasso_rf.csv")
```
## Create training and validation subsets 

```{r}
# Optional test with subset
set.seed(123) 

split <- initial_split(final_train_lags, prop = 0.7)

training_set <- training(split)    # 70% of the data
validation_set <- testing(split)
```

## Fit the Random Forest

```{r}
# Fit random forest model
rf_model <- ranger(
  formula = sales ~ .,                 
  data = training_set,
  min.node.size = 10,
  mtry = 7,
  num.trees = 500,                 
  importance = 'impurity',        
  num.threads = parallel::detectCores(),
  verbose = TRUE
)

# Print model summary
print(rf_model)

# Fit the model on the validation set
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, data = validation_set)

# Output RMSLE on training set
rmsle_val <- rmsle(validation_set$sales, rf_pred$predictions)
print(rmsle_val)

# Save model as a separate object
save(rf_model, file = "rf_model.rda")
```
## Finalize Random Forest predictions and save to .csv

```{r}
# Load random forest model object if necessary

# load("rf_model.rda")

predictions <- predict(rf_model, data = final_test_lags)

pred_val <- cbind(final_test_lags, sales_pred = predictions)

submission <- pred_val %>%
  select(id, prediction) %>%
  rename(sales = prediction)

write.csv(submission, "RF_Model_Submission.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

## Importance Plot

```{r}
# Chech which predictors were most significant

vip(rf_model)

```
